prompt: What do I know about retrieval-augmented generation? I'm trying to remember what sources I have and what the key concepts are.

assertions.name: structured-output-format
assertions.description: Response uses the defined format: synthesized answer, graph path, sources table, adjacent concepts, coverage gaps

assertions.name: graph-traversal-described
assertions.description: Response explicitly describes which folders/files were checked (topics/concepts/, atoms/, sources/)

assertions.name: graceful-empty-handling
assertions.description: If vault has no RAG content, response clearly states this and suggests next steps rather than fabricating connections

assertions.name: no-hallucinated-files
assertions.description: Any [[note links]] cited in the response correspond to files that actually exist in the vault

assertions.name: confidence-signaled
assertions.description: Response signals confidence level based on source status (processed/read/unread or absence of content)
